prompt: The following VCF record has a MNP (multi-nucleotide polymorphism) where both REF and ALT are the same length but differ at multiple positions. Identify the changed bases and their positions relative to the POS field.
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_F01
chr08	14829201	.	TCGA	TAGC	1480	PASS	AF=0.03;AN=6020	GT:DP:GQ	0/1:24:52
correct_answer: This is a MNP where REF=TCGA and ALT=TAGC. Comparing base-by-base: position 14829201 T->T (same), position 14829202 C->A (changed), position 14829203 G->G (same), position 14829204 A->C (changed). Two bases changed: C->A at pos+1 and A->C at pos+3.
rubric: Must identify the two changed positions (14829202 C->A and 14829204 A->C). Must note the unchanged positions. Must recognize this as a MNP (not a simple SNP or indel).
notes: Tests MNP parsing -- a variant type often confused with indels because REF and ALT have multiple bases.
